summary: Align the site mentor-assignment POST route with the existing GET site-default behavior so multi-site site admins can save real assignments without posting siteId.
selectedReason: This was a real route inconsistency with direct workflow impact: GET already inferred the current/default school for site-admin mentor-assignment access, but POST omitted site_admin from the same helper path. Fixing that route bug had more product value than another copy-only pass and stayed safely inside existing RBAC.

candidates.candidate: Site-admin mentor assignment POST default fallback

candidates.decision: not selected: still safe, but the route bug had clearer workflow value
deferred: larger than this route fix
deferred: no backed site audit destination yet

candidates.candidate: Review Queue row-level review affordance clarity
candidates.decision: not selected: narrower impact than the route bug
deferred: hosted fake-account runtime is outside this local batch

changes: Added site_admin to the mentor-assignment POST default-site role list.
Proved a multi-site site admin can create an assignment without posting siteId.
Kept duplicate, cross-site, non-mentor, and unauthorized mutation denials intact.
Updated the audit and role-readiness docs to record the route-level fix.
nextRecommended: Refresh Administration/AP site dashboard language, or add read-only mentor assignment history to student detail.
